description: The same `@.` attribute-value sigil INSIDE an `<each>` body scope is silent — no E-SYNTAX-064 (§17.7.3).
rationale: §17.7.3 normative: `@.` SHALL resolve to the current iteration value of the most-locally-enclosing `` body scope; `@.field` resolves to the field of the current item and chained/member access is legal (the section's own worked example is `${@.email}` inside ``). This case is the exact mirror of the -pos case with the identical `` markup wrapped in an `` body, so it pins the SCOPE BOUNDARY: E-SYNTAX-064 keys on the absence of an enclosing `` body scope, not on the `@.` token itself. `key=@.id` and the `${@.name}` interpolation additionally pin the opener-attribute and interpolation positions as legal inside the body.
